intent: Fix six visual/UX QA issues discovered after library shelf consistency implementation: (1) remove chapter info from Browse audiobook cards, (2) reposition Daily Highlights above format tabs, (3) migrate Discover shelf to LibraryRail+BookTile pattern, (4) switch to icon-only format badges, (5) fix hover overlay icon contrast on light covers, and (6) fix hover-lift animation clipping in scroll containers.
reviewers: correctness, testing, maintainability, project-standards, adversarial, kieran-typescript, agent-native-reviewer, learnings-researcher

reviewer_justifications.adversarial: 9 files changed, ~150+ executable lines across 8 TSX components (>=50 threshold)
reviewer_justifications.kieran-typescript: TypeScript React component changes across multiple library files

residual_risks: Discover shelf migration changes component from LibraryMediaShelfRow to LibraryRail — chevron hover behavior now matches Recently Added (intentional per plan). Visual verification via design review recommended.

BookTile action icon reduced from size-10 (40px) to size-6 (24px) inside p-2 backdrop circle — total footprint unchanged (40px), but icon within is smaller. The backdrop circle pattern is proven in BookCard's format badge.
testing_gaps: RecentBookCard format badge change (text removal, icon-only) has no test coverage — no RecentBookCard.test.tsx exists
BookTile dark backdrop circle (bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-md rounded-full) wrapping action icon has no explicit test in overlay test suite
Discover shelf BookTile rendering not explicitly verified in LibraryMediaShelfColumn tests (existing assertions use >=1 which pass but don't distinguish Discover children from other shelves)
Scroll container pt-2 additions have no className-level regression tests (visual verification is appropriate for CSS changes)
learnings: Preceding solution doc: docs/solutions/ui-bugs/library-shelf-sizing-hover-consistency-2026-05-05.md — documents the translateZ(0) corner-clipping fix and unified sizing pattern from plan 005. This plan addresses 6 post-unification QA follow-ups.
